I could chase Timber or Fiber, but why scramble when I can secure the foundation of industry right now? Ore is the backbone of the Forge and the Watchtower, the structures that truly secure an empire. I'll gather at home, securing 2 Ore. I'll float a trade offer to swap 1F for 1T or 1O to see who bites. Meanwhile, I need to get off zero. Plains is open, Timber is the universal building block. I'll gather there to start the flow.

I can't build yet, and I can't ambush without Scrap. I must gather. Plains offers Timber, which pairs perfectly with my Ore for a future Shack. I'll float a trade to P3 to swap my F for their O—standard swap to diversify. If they bite, great; if not, I still gather. I'm hitting Mountains to grab Ore. Building a Den (1F + 1T + 1S) is my immediate target, so I need that Ore. Scrap is low priority right now; I need the resource density.

P4 is holding a banquet of resources but starving for Ore to build. I have a surplus of 4 Ore. Trading 1 Ore for 1 Fiber is a steal—it diversifies my hand and adds a partner to my ledger, which is crucial for tiebreaks. I'll accept that deal immediately. With my hand now holding Timber, Ore, and Fiber, I'm positioned to build a Forge or Den soon, but I need Scrap. I'll head to the Ruins to gather Scrap, keeping my engine warm and my options open.
